creative_brief: Hero image: close-up of a hand holding a vacuum-sealed steak with a clear farm-name label (e.g. 'Rachel's Farm, Kempsey NSW') beside a generic supermarket pack with no origin info. Split-screen contrast. Warm, natural lighting on the FarmThru side. Cool, fluorescent tone on the supermarket side. Earthy brand palette throughout.
headline: We traced 9 cuts of supermarket beef. Zero named the farm.
subhead: FarmThru puts the farmer's name on every pack. 100% grass-fed. Collected fresh from your local hub.
hero_copy: We wanted to know where grocery store beef actually comes from. So we checked. Nine different cuts from three major chains. Not one label named a farm. Not one told you how far it travelled. Industry data suggests that journey averages 2,400 kilometres. FarmThru is a different kind of grocery store. Every product traces to a named regenerative farm. You will know exactly what you are eating.

sections.heading: What 2,400 kilometres does to your food
sections.body:
Supermarket beef can spend 14 to 21 days in cold storage before it reaches the shelf. That is not fresh. That is logistics.

FarmThru sources straight from farm to hub to your kitchen. 100% grass-fed. Pasture-raised. No hormones, no antibiotics, no feedlot finishing.

You collect from your nearest hub. Brookvale is open Monday to Friday. The difference is not subtle. You will taste it in the first bite.

sections.heading: You will know every farmer by name
sections.body:
Rachel Ward runs a regenerative farm in Kempsey, NSW. She is a key partner and supplier to FarmThru.

Bundarra Farm provides pork belly and pork and fennel salami. Collins Wild Caught brings wild-caught Alaskan salmon from the South Coast of NSW. Farmer Brown supplies eggs. Paris Creek Farms in Meadows, SA delivers carbon-neutral biodynamic dairy.

Six farms. Real names. Real places. That is what 100% sourced from regenerative farms actually looks like.

sections.heading: How it works: 3 steps to better food
sections.body:
1. Browse the shop online. Regenerative beef, pasture-raised chicken, wild-caught salmon, heritage pork, eggs, and dairy.

2. Place your order. No subscription. No commitment. Order when you want.

3. Collect from your nearest hub. Brookvale hub is open Monday to Friday. Straight from farm to hub, ready for your kitchen.

sections.heading: Own a piece of the grocery store that pays farmers first
sections.body:
FarmThru is raising funds through equity crowdfunding. This is your chance to be part of building a farm-direct grocery store from the ground up.

Join the waitlist with a $5 refundable deposit. That small step puts you at the front of the line when the campaign opens. Over 2,500 people have already signed up.

You can be a customer, a partner, or both. Values and money finally pointing the same direction.

Investing in equity crowdfunding carries risk. You may lose some or all of your investment. FarmThru is an early-stage business. Returns are not guaranteed.

sections.heading: Compliance
sections.body: FarmThru's equity crowdfunding campaign is conducted under the Australian equity crowdfunding framework. Investing in early-stage companies carries inherent risks, including the potential loss of your entire investment. Past performance is not indicative of future results. No returns are projected or guaranteed. You should consider whether this investment is appropriate for your financial situation and seek independent financial advice if needed. The $5 VIP deposit is fully refundable and does not constitute an investment or obligation to invest.
